section_name: Department procedures upon verification of suspension of driving privileges of wrong person
chapter_title: Driving Under the Influence of Intoxicants
text:
If the Department of Transportation verifies to its satisfaction that it has suspended the driving privileges of the wrong person under ORS 813.410 (Suspension upon receipt of police report on implied consent test) because a person arrested for driving under the influence of intoxicants gave false identification at the time of the arrest, all the following apply:

(1) The department shall immediately rescind the suspension order under the false name and shall issue a suspension order for the period set forth in ORS 813.420 (Duration of suspension for refusal or failure of test) to the person arrested.

(2) The department shall issue the order in the manner set forth in ORS 809.430 (Notice of suspension, cancellation or revocation) .

(3) No further notice of suspension need be given.

(4) The time limitations in ORS 813.410 (Suspension upon receipt of police report on implied consent test) (1), (2), (3), (4) and (5)(e) do not apply to a suspension order issued under this section. [1985 c.672 §25; 1989 c.636 §47; 2021 c.630 §119]
markdown:
## Title 59
### Chapter 813. Driving Under the Influence of Intoxicants
##### § 813.460 — Department procedures upon verification of suspension of driving privileges of wrong person

If the Department of Transportation verifies to its satisfaction that it has suspended the driving privileges of the wrong person under ORS 813.410 (Suspension upon receipt of police report on implied consent test) because a person arrested for driving under the influence of intoxicants gave false identification at the time of the arrest, all the following apply:

(1) The department shall immediately rescind the suspension order under the false name and shall issue a suspension order for the period set forth in ORS 813.420 (Duration of suspension for refusal or failure of test) to the person arrested.

(2) The department shall issue the order in the manner set forth in ORS 809.430 (Notice of suspension, cancellation or revocation) .

(3) No further notice of suspension need be given.

(4) The time limitations in ORS 813.410 (Suspension upon receipt of police report on implied consent test) (1), (2), (3), (4) and (5)(e) do not apply to a suspension order issued under this section. [1985 c.672 §25; 1989 c.636 §47; 2021 c.630 §119]

subsections.text: If the Department of Transportation verifies to its satisfaction that it has suspended the driving privileges of the wrong person under ORS 813.410 (Suspension upon receipt of police report on implied consent test) because a person arrested for driving under the influence of intoxicants gave false identification at the time of the arrest, all the following apply:

The department shall immediately rescind the suspension order under the false name and shall issue a suspension order for the period set forth in ORS 813.420 (Duration of suspension for refusal or failure of test) to the person arrested.
The department shall issue the order in the manner set forth in ORS 809.430 (Notice of suspension, cancellation or revocation) .
No further notice of suspension need be given.

The time limitations in ORS 813.410 (Suspension upon receipt of police report on implied consent test) (1), (2), (3), (4) and (5)(e) do not apply to a suspension order issued under this section. [1985 c.672 §25; 1989 c.636 §47; 2021 c.630 §119]
